source_jobs.title: Senior Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Opportunity



The $1.2T automotive industry is a pillar of American culture, representing nearly 5% of U.S. GDP and shaping how people live and move every day. At the center of this ecosystem are dealerships—trusted local businesses that help consumers navigate one of the most important purchases they make.



Today, the industry is at an inflection point. Consumers expect the same speed, personalization, and convenience they experience in modern digital-first industries. At the same time, tighter margins and rising costs are driving a need for operational efficiency beyond what traditional software can support. Together, these shifts create a powerful opportunity to reimagine how dealerships deliver seamless experiences and build lasting customer relationships.



Dealers who modernize how they communicate and operate will unlock efficiency, deepen loyalty, and win long-term relationships. It is time to upgrade automotive retail to the 21st century.



About Mia

Mia Labs is an investor-backed startup on a mission to modernize dealership operations and transform the automotive consumer experience. Our AI agent, Mia, turns every call or inquiry into an opportunity, improving customer satisfaction and dealership performance through automation that feels human. We are trusted by some of the nation's largest dealership groups and are rapidly scaling our team, product offerings, and customer base.



About the Role:

We are looking for an experienced Senior Product Engineer to join our Product team. This role emphasizes backend Python development, good system design, and the deployment of scalable systems — with a strong focus on the voice and multi-channel communication technology at the core of what Mia does. You will work closely with our AI lead to push the boundaries of what's possible, while contributing to the product roadmap as priorities evolve.



What You Will Do:



 - Design, develop, and maintain scalable backend systems using Python.

 - Build and contribute to frontend features using TypeScript and React.

 - Improve voice pipeline performance, expanding multi-channel communication capabilities, and evaluating emerging technologies.

 - Implement and optimize real-time speech-to-text (STT) and text-to-speech (TTS) systems.

 - Contribute to multi-channel messaging systems (SMS, email, voice) built on a shared agentic backbone.

 - Own complex refactoring and architecture projects —replacing brittle, legacy patterns with clean, testable, scalable solutions.

 - Apply best practices in software development, including testing, CI/CD pipelines, and code reviews.

 - Mentor and guide team members, fostering technical growth and collaboration.
   
   

What We Want You to Have:



 - 5+ years of professional software engineering experience with strong Python fundamentals.

 - Experience with frontend development using TypeScript and React.

 - A product mindset — you think about the "why" behind what you build and can contribute meaningfully to roadmap conversations.

 - Proven ability to lead large-scale refactoring or architecture migration projects — shipping incrementally without breaking production.

 - Experience deploying applications in cloud environments (AWS, GCP, or Azure) using Docker.

 - Strong communication and ownership; comfortable working directly with senior cross-functional stakeholders.

 - Use AI to code — Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, or equivalent. This is how we work.


WHY MIA

 - Equity participation through stock options.

 - Fully paid health, vision & dental insurance for employees.

 - Flexible PTO and hybrid schedule (3 days / week in downtown Austin).

 - Free lunches, parking stipend provided, team events, and a casual, but get things done culture.


COMPENSATION

 - Compensation for this position will be competitive with current market rates and reflective of the candidate's experience, skills and qualifications.


OUR COMMITMENT

At Mia, we’re committed to a workplace where everyo

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer (AI/ML)
source_jobs.description:
The Opportunity



The $1.2T automotive industry is a pillar of American culture, representing nearly 5% of U.S. GDP and shaping how people live and move every day. At the center of this ecosystem are dealerships—trusted local businesses that help consumers navigate one of the most important purchases they make.



Today, the industry is at an inflection point. Consumers expect the same speed, personalization, and convenience they experience in modern digital-first industries. At the same time, tighter margins and rising costs are driving a need for operational efficiency beyond what traditional software can support. Together, these shifts create a powerful opportunity to reimagine how dealerships deliver seamless experiences and build lasting customer relationships.



Dealers who modernize how they communicate and operate will unlock efficiency, deepen loyalty, and win long-term relationships. It is time to upgrade automotive retail to the 21st century.



About Mia

Mia Labs is an investor-backed startup on a mission to modernize dealership operations and transform the automotive consumer experience. Our AI agent, Mia, turns every call or inquiry into an opportunity, improving customer satisfaction and dealership performance through automation that feels human. We are trusted by some of the nation's largest dealership groups and are rapidly scaling our team, product offerings, and customer base.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring a Software Engineer (AI/ML) to work on our voice AI platform, the real-time pipeline of speech-to-text, LLM reasoning, and text-to-speech that powers every call. You'll work on latency, model quality, cost, and reliability across the full AI stack, and help shape which models and vendors we bet on. This is a deep, focused role on a small core product team, with direct mentorship from our AI lead.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and improve the real-time voice AI pipeline: speech-to-text, conversational LLM orchestration, and text-to-speech, with hard latency targets (we hold P90 end-to-end latency under 2 seconds and are pushing lower).

 - Improve model performance in production, run evals for core functionality

 - Improve instrumentation (OTel/Braintrust), build dashboards and alerting

 - Optimize cost: squeezing more throughput out of open source LLMs

 - Work with vendors like Anthropic, Microsoft/Azure OpenAI, Deepgram, and NVIDIA — testing new voice and language models, evaluating alternatives, and handling failover when a provider has issues.

 - Ship AI R&D features into production: things like backchanneling, emotional expressiveness, background audio, voice cloning, and multilingual features

 - Implement AI guardrails so models behave correctly on live customer calls
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 1+ years of experience in software development (including relevant internships).

 - Strong engineering fundamentals: you write production code, instrument it, and own what you ship

 - Comfort with observability tooling (OpenTelemetry or similar) and making decisions from metrics

 - Scrappy, startup-ready mindset: small team, broad ownership, fast iteration

 - Clear communicator — you'll explain AI behavior and tradeoffs to product, support, and customer-facing teams
   
   


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with real-time audio or voice AI (STT/TTS pipelines, streaming, WebRTC/networking, telephony)

 - Hands-on experience building with LLMs in production: prompt design, model evaluation, latency/cost optimization

 - Background in speech/audio ML (a founder or early builder of a voice product is a great signal — that's how our current Head of AI got here)

 - Azure cloud experience


WHY MIA

 - Equity participation through stock options.

 - Fully paid health, vision & dental insurance for employees.

 - Flexible PTO and hybrid schedule (3 days / week in downtown Austin).

 - Free lunches, parking stipend provided, team events, and a casual, but get things done culture.


COMPENSATION

 -

source_jobs.title: Senior Solutions Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Opportunity



The $1.2T automotive industry is a pillar of American culture, representing nearly 5% of U.S. GDP and shaping how people live and move every day. At the center of this ecosystem are dealerships—trusted local businesses that help consumers navigate one of the most important purchases they make.



Today, the industry is at an inflection point. Consumers expect the same speed, personalization, and convenience they experience in modern digital-first industries. At the same time, tighter margins and rising costs are driving a need for operational efficiency beyond what traditional software can support. Together, these shifts create a powerful opportunity to reimagine how dealerships deliver seamless experiences and build lasting customer relationships.



Dealers who modernize how they communicate and operate will unlock efficiency, deepen loyalty, and win long-term relationships. It is time to upgrade automotive retail to the 21st century.



About Mia

Mia Labs is an investor-backed startup on a mission to modernize dealership operations and transform the automotive consumer experience. Our AI agent, Mia, turns every call or inquiry into an opportunity, improving customer satisfaction and dealership performance through automation that feels human. We are trusted by some of the nation's largest dealership groups and are rapidly scaling our team, product offerings, and customer base.




ABOUT THE ROLE:

We're looking for a Senior Solutions Engineer to join our Client Solutions Engineering team. This is a high-ownership role that blends product engineering, integrations work, and hands-on client problem-solving. You'll build and maintain the systems that connect Mia to dealership platforms (DMS, service schedulers, CRMs), own production changes end-to-end, and occasionally sit across the table from dealer groups to launch and troubleshoot in real time.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design, build, and ship features across our platform, including our third-party integrations and internal tooling that makes the rest of the company faster.

 - Own production changes end-to-end: scope the work in Jira, drive peer and lead review, and verify results in production.

 - Respond to production alerts and incidents, diagnose root causes, and fix fast — all while keeping impacted clients informed.

 - Support dealer onboarding and launches: collaborate with Account Managers and dealer group personnel to set up integrations, make changes to phone systems, and test the product ahead of go-live.

 - Partner directly with client-facing teams (sales, account management) as an escalation point — jumping on client calls when needed and helping teammates navigate tough technical conversations.

 - Represent engineering with clients: occasional on-site visits and presentations to dealer groups during pilots and rollouts.

 - Improve how the team works — contribute to the creation and rollout of better procedures, safer change processes, and tooling that prevents mistakes rather than just fixing them.
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of professional software engineering experience with strong Python fundamentals and relational database experience (PostgreSQL preferred).

 - Experience building and supporting third-party API integrations, ideally in a B2B SaaS environment.

 - Comfort operating in a live-ops environment: on-call / alert response, careful change management, and verifying your own work.

 - Strong communication skills — you can explain technical issues to non-technical clients and internal teams with patience and clarity.

 - Bias toward ownership: you follow issues through to resolution and keep stakeholders posted along the way.

 - Willingness to travel occasionally for on-site client visits.

 - Use AI to code — Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, or equivalent. This is how we work.
   
   


NICE TO HAVE

 - Automotive retail / dealership software experience (DMS, service scheduling, CRM platforms).

 - Cloud infra

notes: All 3 postings are tech roles, with a focus on automotive industry disruption.
